name: Université Antonine

admissions_process.summary: Graduate applicants apply through the Office of Orientation and Admissions, submit the required file to the relevant faculty, meet faculty-specific admission criteria, and complete any interview/entrance-exam steps required by the program.
steps: Review the graduate program and its faculty-specific admission criteria.
Prepare the required documents and submit the application file through admissions.
Complete any required interview, selection criteria, or entrance exam steps.
Finish enrollment and fee payment according to the faculty calendar and instructions.

required_documents.summary: UA publishes a general graduate checklist plus program-specific or transfer-related documents where applicable.
items: Civil-status / ID copy for Lebanese students
Lebanese Baccalaureate or equivalent certified by MEHE
Bachelor degree or equivalent certified by MEHE
Transfer records and course descriptions when applicable
Family status record or equivalent civil document where required

language_requirements.summary: No university-wide graduate language threshold was published in the reviewed sources; language expectations are faculty- and program-specific.
notes: The Language Center provides placement/testing and remedial support in English and French.

tuition_model.summary: UA publishes per-credit graduate tuition by faculty/program in the graduate tuition page and Fall 2026-27 tuition PDF. All mapped program tuition is stored on the program records; the theology programs did not expose a reliable title-level tuition value in the accessible official source set.
tuition_model.notes: Music and Musicology pages show a collective-course price of $200 and an individual-course price of $245; the program records store the $200 collective rate.

tuition_table.notes: ASB MBA programs publish the same $230 credit price.
Official page shows collective course $200 and individual course $245; the program records store the collective rate.

application_fee.notes: Graduate application fee published on the application and required-documents pages and echoed in the fee PDF.

entrance_exam_fee.notes: Graduate entrance exam fee published on the graduate admissions pages and fee PDF.

registration_fee.notes: The fee sheet lists fixed registration fees, but the OCR extraction did not expose a single universal graduate registration amount.

student_id_card_fee.notes: UA Smart Student ID Card fee published in the fee sheet.

insurance_fee.notes: Individual accident insurance / NSSF-related fee appears in the fee sheet, but the extracted text is not clean enough to model a single universal amount confidently.

notes: UA publishes fees by semester/faculty in the official fee sheet.
No graduate installment plan was published in the reviewed sources.

scholarships.summary: No university-wide graduate scholarship table was published in the reviewed official sources. External or doctoral-oriented scholarship announcements exist, but they are not a general graduate scholarship policy.

financial_aid.summary: UA social-affairs aid is undergraduate-only in the reviewed rules; no broad graduate aid catalogue was published.

payment_plans.summary: No graduate installment plan was published; the reviewed fee sheet and admissions materials do not describe a graduate payment-plan schedule.

academic_calendar.summary: UA publishes an official academic calendar with registration, enrollment, payment, withdrawal, exams, and results dates.

graduate_regulations.summary: Students must follow the relevant faculty regulations and university rules; no centralized graduate handbook was identified in the reviewed sources.

accreditation.summary: The reviewed sources include an official accreditation certificate for the Master of Music and Musicology. No broader graduate accreditation catalogue was identified.

international_students.summary: Foreign students can enroll with a certified passport copy and may use an authorized representative or embassy POA path if they cannot attend in person.

language_policy.summary: No university-wide graduate language policy was published in the reviewed sources; language expectations remain faculty- and program-specific.

notes: UA-SRC-059 and UA-SRC-060 are not applicable here; the shared graduate fee model is unique to UL.
Program tuition is mapped only where a title-level official rate was visible in the reviewed UA sources.
Theology program tuition was not mapped because a reliable title-level rate was not exposed in the accessible official source set.
